title: [lora_l2.c:1010] lichen_lora_l2_set_rx_callback returns void, cannot report errors
description:
Contract: lichen_lora_l2_set_rx_callback should set callback atomically.
Violation: The function returns void, so it cannot report the early-return case where state == LORA_UNINIT (line 1012). Caller has no way to know the callback was not set.
Impact: If called before init(), the callback registration silently fails. Caller may believe RX is enabled when it is not. The LOG_WRN provides some visibility but is not programmatic.
Mitigation: Change return type to int and return -ENODEV when not initialized. Audit callers to check return value.
close_reason: Fixed: Changed lichen_lora_l2_set_rx_callback return type from void to int. Returns -ENODEV when not initialized, 0 on success. Updated header documentation. All 4 callers in lichen_l2.c now handle return value (2 with error checks, 2 cast to void in cleanup paths). Caller in main.c now checks return value and returns error on failure.
